_meta.description: Timed lunar eclipse observations from Stephenson, Morrison & Hohenkerk (2016), Proc. Roy. Soc. A 472:20160404 — supplementary tables S01, S02, S04, S05, S07, S09. Each row is a single primary-source observation reduced by the Stephenson team to an implied ΔT value (seconds). Phase L-5b uses this to compare our pure-tidal ΔT model and NASA's Espenak/Meeus polynomial directly against observation-derived ΔT.
_meta.source_paper: Stephenson, F.R., Morrison, L.V., Hohenkerk, C.Y. (2016) Proc. Roy. Soc. A 472:20160404

tables_included.S02: Babylonian lunar eclipses, culmination of ziqpu star
tables_included.S04: Babylonian lunar eclipses in the Almagest
tables_included.S07: Greek timed lunar and solar eclipses

field_definitions.source_table: Table id in Stephenson supplementary (S01, S02, S04, S05, S07, S09).
field_definitions.year: Calendar year of observation (negative for BCE; proleptic Julian).
field_definitions.dt_observed_sec: ΔT in seconds derived by Stephenson from this observation. NULL for S04 bounds-only entries.
field_definitions.weight: Observation weight assigned by Stephenson (higher = more reliable; 0 = lowest). NULL for bounds-only.
field_definitions.degree: S01 only: umbral magnitude (or qualitative '?' suffix).
field_definitions.measurement: S04 only: narrative description of how the contact was timed.
field_definitions.dt_lower_sec: S04 bounds rows only: ΔT lower limit (s).
field_definitions.dt_upper_sec: S04 bounds rows only: ΔT upper limit (s).
field_definitions.note: S04 bounds rows only: source note.
